segments.text: In the beginning the Word already was.  The Word was with God, and the Word was God.

footnotes.text: + In other words, the Word existed from eternity past. The concept of the Word means more than letters making up a word: it is the divine mind, the expression of God, the active aspect of divinity that speaks into existence as in Genesis 1:1.

segments.text: From the very beginning he was with God.
Everything came into being through him; nothing came into being without him.
In him was life, the life that was the light of everyone.
The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not extinguished it.

footnotes.text: + The word in the original can also mean {   "type": "speech",   "text": "overpowered" }  or  {   "type": "speech",   "text": "understood." }

segments.text: God sent a man named John.
He came as a witness to explain about the light so that everyone might believe through him.
He himself was not the light, but he came to witness to the light.
The true light was coming into the world to give light to everyone.
He was in the world, and though the world was made through him, the world didn't know who he was.
He came to his own people, but they didn't accept him.

footnotes.text: + Or {   "type": "speech",   "text": "He came to his own home but his own people did not welcome him." }

segments.text: But to all those who accepted him and trusted in him, he gave the right to become God's children.
These are the children born not in the usual way, not as the result of human desire or a father's decision, but born of God.
The Word became human and lived among us, and we saw his glory, the glory of the Father's one and only Son, full of grace and truth.

footnotes.text: + Literally, {   "type": "speech",   "text": "only begotten." } This refers to position and uniqueness rather than birth.

segments.text: John gave his testimony about him, shouting out to the people, {
This is the one I was telling you about when I said, ‘The one who is coming after me is more important than me, for before I ever existed he already was.’
segments.speaker: John

segments.text: We have all been recipients of his generous nature, one gracious gift after another.
For the law was given through Moses; grace and truth came through Jesus Christ.
While no one has ever seen God, God the one and only, who is close beside the Father, has shown us what God is like.
This is what John publicly stated when the Jewish leaders sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, {
Who are you?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: John declared plainly and clearly without hesitation, {
I am not the Messiah.
segments.speaker: John

segments.text: So then, who are you?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they asked.
Elijah?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: No, I'm not,
segments.speaker: John

segments.text: he answered.
Are you the Prophet?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: No,
segments.speaker: John

segments.text: he replied.

footnotes.text: + In Jewish thought a special prophet was expected before the End.

segments.text: Well, who are you, then?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they asked.
We have to give an answer to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: I am a voice calling in the desert, ‘Make the Lord's way straight!’
segments.speaker: John

segments.text: he said, using the words of the prophet Isaiah.
The priests and Levites sent by the Pharisees
asked him, {
Why then are you baptizing, if you're not the Messiah, or Elijah, or the Prophet?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: John replied, {
I baptize with water, but standing among you is someone you don't know.
segments.speaker: John

segments.text: He is coming after me, but I am not even worthy to untie his sandals.
segments.speaker: John

segments.text: This all happened in Bethany beyond the Jordan, where John was baptizing.
The next day John saw Jesus approaching him, and said, {
Look, the Lamb of God, who takes away the sin of the world!
segments.speaker: John

segments.text: This is the one I was talking about when I said, ‘A man who is coming after me is more important than me, for before I ever existed he already was.’
segments.speaker: John

segments.text: I didn't know myself who he was, but I came baptizing with water so that he could be revealed to Israel.
segments.speaker: John

segments.text: John gave his evidence about him, saying, {
I saw the Spirit descend from heaven like a dove and rest upon him.
segments.speaker: John

segments.text: I wouldn't have known him except he who sent me to baptize with water had told me, ‘The one you see the Spirit descend to and rest upon, he is the one who baptizes with the Holy Spirit.’
segments.speaker: John

segments.text: I saw it happen and I declare that this is the Son of God.
segments.speaker: John

segments.text: The next day John was standing there with two of his disciples.
He saw Jesus passing by, and said, {
Look! This is the Lamb of God!
segments.speaker: John

segments.text: When the two disciples heard what he said they went and followed Jesus.
Jesus turned round and saw them following him. {
What are you looking for?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: he asked them.
Rabbi (which means ‘Teacher’), where are you staying?
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: they asked in reply.
Come and see,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: he told them. So they went with him and saw where he was staying. It was about four p. m., and they spent the rest of the day with him.
Andrew, the brother of Simon Peter, was one of these two disciples who had heard what John said and followed Jesus.
He went at once to find his brother Simon and told him, {
We've found the Messiah!
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: (which means
Christ
segments.speaker: Unknown

footnotes.text: + Both Messiah and Christ mean {   "type": "speech",   "text": "the Anointed One." }

segments.text: He took him to Jesus. Looking directly at Simon, Jesus said, {
You are Simon, son of John. But now you will be called Cephas,
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: (which means
Peter
segments.speaker: Jesus

footnotes.text: + Cephas and Peter both mean {   "type": "speech",   "text": "rock" }  or  {   "type": "speech",   "text": "stone." }

segments.text: The next day Jesus decided to go to Galilee. Jesus found Philip there, and told him, {
Follow me.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Philip was from Bethsaida, the same town that Andrew and Peter came from.
Philip found Nathanael and told him, {
We've found the one that Moses wrote about in the law and that the prophets did too—Jesus of Nazareth, the son of Joseph.
segments.speaker: Philip found Nathanael and

segments.text: From Nazareth? Can anything good come from there?
segments.speaker: Philip found Nathanael and

segments.text: Nathanael wondered.
Just come and see,
segments.speaker: Philip

segments.text: Philip replied.
As Jesus saw Nathanael approaching, he said about him, {
Look, here's a true Israelite! There's nothing false about him.
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: How do you know who I am?
segments.speaker: Nathanael

segments.text: Nathanael asked.
I saw you there under the fig tree, before Philip called you,
segments.speaker: Nathanael

segments.text: Jesus replied.
Rabbi, you are the Son of God, the king of Israel!
segments.speaker: Nathaniel

segments.text: Nathaniel exclaimed.
You believe this just because I told you I saw you under the fig tree?
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Jesus replied.
You'll get to see much more than that!
segments.speaker: Jesus

segments.text: Then Jesus said, {
I tell you the truth, you will all see heaven open, and the angels of God going up and down on the Son of man.
segments.speaker: Jesus

footnotes.text: + Referring to Jacob's experience in Genesis 28:12, with the term {   "type": "speech",   "text": "Son of man" }  replacing the word  {   "type": "speech",   "text": "ladder." }
